name: Number System & HCF/LCM
description: Divisibility rules, shortcuts and fast calculation tricks

sections.title: Divisibility Rules — Learn Once, Use Forever
HCF — Fast Methods
formulas: HCF of fractions = HCF of Numerators / LCM of Denominators
shortcuts: METHOD 1 (Factorization): Find common prime factors, take lowest power of each.
METHOD 2 (Division): Divide bigger by smaller, then smaller by remainder. Keep dividing until remainder = 0. Last divisor = HCF.

sections.title: LCM — Fast Methods
formulas: LCM of fractions = LCM of Numerators / HCF of Denominators
HCF × LCM = Product of two numbers (only for 2 numbers)
shortcuts: METHOD: Find all prime factors, take HIGHEST power of each.

sections.title: Number Tricks for SSC Speed
shortcuts:
Rule: For n5² → multiply n×(n+1), attach 25 at end
    75² → 7×8 = 56 → answer = 5625
    85² → 8×9 = 72 → answer = 7225

sections.title: Remainders & Cyclicity Tricks
shortcuts:
Trick: Find (power ÷ cycle length) → use remainder to get position in cycle
    Last digit of 7²³ → cycle of 4 → 23÷4 = remainder 3 → 3rd in cycle = 3

When dividing aⁿ by (a+1): Remainder is always 1 (if n is even) or a (if n is odd)
    Example: 7⁵ ÷ 8 → 7 is odd power → remainder = 7

sections.title: Important Number System Facts
sections.content:
Natural: 1,2,3... (No 0)
Whole: 0,1,2,3...
Integers: ...-2,-1,0,1,2...
Rational: p/q form (q≠0)
Prime: Only 1 and itself as factors
Co-prime: HCF = 1
formulas:
Sum of 1 to n = n(n+1)/2
Sum of odd numbers 1 to n = ((n+1)/2)²
Sum of squares = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6
Sum of cubes = [n(n+1)/2]²

name: Percentage & Ratio/Proportion
description: The most important topics — used in every other chapter

sections.title: % Increase/Decrease
formulas:
% Change = (Change/Original) × 100

If A is x% more than B:
B is x/(100+x) × 100% less than A

If A is x% less than B:
B is x/(100-x) × 100% more than A
shortcuts:
Shortcut: 20% more → multiply by 1.2
      20% less → multiply by 0.8
      30% more then 10% less → 1.3 × 0.9 = 1.17 = 17% net increase

sections.title: Successive % Change
formulas:
Net % = a + b + (ab/100)
where a, b are the two percentages
(use – for decrease)

sections.title: Population & Depreciation
formulas:
Population after n years = P × (1 + r/100)ⁿ
Depreciation after n years = P × (1 - r/100)ⁿ
shortcuts: Fast: For small r and n=2: use successive % formula twice. Saves time.

sections.title: Ratio & Proportion Shortcuts
formulas:
If a:b = m:n and b:c = p:q
Then a:b:c = mp : np : nq

Componendo: (a+b)/(a-b) = (c+d)/(c-d) if a/b = c/d

Fourth Proportional to a, b, c = bc/a
Third Proportional to a, b = b²/a
Mean Proportional to a, b = √(ab)
shortcuts:
Alligation Cross:
      Cheaper ——— Mean ——— Dearer
      Ratio = (Dearer - Mean) : (Mean - Cheaper)
Example: Mix milk at ₹20/L and ₹30/L to get ₹24/L
      Ratio = (30-24):(24-20) = 6:4 = 3:2

sections.title: Partnership Problems
formulas:
Profit share ∝ Capital × Time

If A invests Ca for Ta months and B invests Cb for Tb months:
Ratio of profits = Ca×Ta : Cb×Tb

name: Profit, Loss & Discount
description: Every formula you need with fast-solving tricks

sections.title: Core Formulas
formulas:
Profit = SP - CP
Loss = CP - SP
Profit% = (Profit/CP) × 100
Loss% = (Loss/CP) × 100

SP = CP × (100+P%)/100  [Profit]
SP = CP × (100-L%)/100  [Loss]
CP = SP × 100/(100+P%)  [find CP from SP]
CP = SP × 100/(100-L%)  [find CP from SP]

sections.title: Dishonest Trader Trick
formulas:
If trader uses false weight w instead of true weight W:
Profit% = (W-w)/w × 100

sections.title: Two Article Trick
formulas:
Sell 2 items at same price, one at x% profit, one at x% loss:
Net Result = ALWAYS LOSS
Loss% = x²/100

sections.title: Discount & Marked Price
formulas:
Discount = MP - SP
Discount% = (Discount/MP) × 100
SP = MP × (100 - D%)/100

Successive discounts of a% and b%:
Net discount = a + b - ab/100
shortcuts:
Fast Trick: MP = SP × 100/(100-D%)
    CP to MP with P% profit after D% discount:
    MP/CP = (100+P%)/(100-D%)

sections.title: Buying & Selling More/Less Trick
formulas:
Buy n items at price of m (i.e., sell m, get n):
Profit% = (n-m)/m × 100 (if n > m)
shortcuts: If buy x items for ₹ and sell x items for ₹ (different amounts):

name: Simple & Compound Interest
description: Most formula-heavy topic — master these shortcuts to save time

sections.title: Simple Interest
shortcuts: Trick: If SI on a sum for 3 years is ₹X and for 5 years is ₹Y, then Rate = (Y-X)/(P×2) × 100. Just find extra SI for 2 years → get principal.

sections.title: Compound Interest
formulas:
A = P × (1 + R/100)ⁿ
CI = A - P

Half-yearly: A = P × (1 + R/200)²ⁿ
Quarterly: A = P × (1 + R/400)⁴ⁿ
shortcuts:
For 2 years: CI = P × [R²/10000 + 2R/100]
Difference: CI - SI (2 years) = PR²/10000 = SI × R/100

sections.title: Installment Problems
formulas:
For equal installments x paying off P at R% SI over n years:
P = x×n - SI on [x×n(n-1)/2] at rate R

For CI: Each installment = A/(1+R/100) + A/(1+R/100)² + ...
shortcuts:
Rule of 72: Time to double money at R% = 72/R years (approximate)
    At 8% → doubles in ≈ 9 years
    At 12% → doubles in ≈ 6 years

name: Time & Work + Pipes & Cisterns
description: Think in terms of "work done per day" — always use fractions

sections.title: Time & Work — Core Approach
formulas:
If A does in a days, B does in b days:
Together = ab/(a+b) days

A, B, C together = 1/(1/a + 1/b + 1/c) days

If A is twice as fast as B: ratio of work = 2:1, ratio of time = 1:2
shortcuts:
GOLDEN RULE: If A does work in n days → A's 1 day work = 1/n
      If A and B together → (1/a + 1/b) work per day → Together complete in ab/(a+b) days

sections.title: Work & Wages
formulas: Wages are divided in ratio of work done (= ratio of efficiency)

sections.title: Men × Days × Hours
formulas:
M₁×D₁×H₁/W₁ = M₂×D₂×H₂/W₂
M=Men, D=Days, H=Hours, W=Work

sections.title: Pipes & Cisterns
formulas:
Two inlets (a and b hours) + one outlet (c hours):
Net filling rate = 1/a + 1/b - 1/c
Time to fill = 1/(net rate)
shortcuts:
Inlet pipe fills: Work = +1/n per hour
Outlet pipe empties: Work = -1/n per hour
      Net work = Sum of all (with signs)

sections.title: Alternate Work Tricks
shortcuts:
If A alone takes 'a' days and A+B takes 't' days:
    B alone = at/(a-t) days
Efficiency Method (LCM trick): Assume total work = LCM of all days. Then work per day = LCM/days. Fastest method for SSC!

If A and B work alternately (A starts):
    Find work in 2 days → how many complete pairs? Then check remaining.

name: Speed, Distance & Time + Trains + Boats
description: Three topics, one core idea — master all cases

sections.title: Core Formula & Conversions
formulas:
Speed = Distance / Time
Distance = Speed × Time
Time = Distance / Speed

km/h → m/s: multiply by 5/18
m/s → km/h: multiply by 18/5
shortcuts: Quick conversions: 18 km/h = 5 m/s | 36 km/h = 10 m/s | 54 km/h = 15 m/s | 72 km/h = 20 m/s

sections.title: Average Speed
formulas:
If same distance at speeds a and b:
Avg Speed = 2ab/(a+b) [Harmonic Mean]

If same TIME at speeds a and b:
Avg Speed = (a+b)/2 [Arithmetic Mean]

sections.title: Relative Speed
formulas:
Same direction: Relative Speed = |S₁ - S₂|
Opposite direction: Relative Speed = S₁ + S₂

sections.title: Trains — All Cases
shortcuts: Key tip: A "person" has zero length. A "stationary object" has zero speed.

sections.title: Boats & Streams
formulas:
U = Speed of boat in still water
V = Speed of stream (current)

Downstream speed = U + V
Upstream speed = U - V

U = (Downstream + Upstream) / 2
V = (Downstream - Upstream) / 2
shortcuts:
Trick: If boat covers same distance upstream in time t₁ and downstream in t₂:
      U:V = (t₁+t₂):(t₁-t₂)

sections.title: Meeting Point Problems
formulas:
Two people A(speed a) & B(speed b) start from opposite ends (dist D):
Meeting time = D/(a+b)
At meeting: A covers = D×a/(a+b), B covers = D×b/(a+b)
shortcuts: Shortcut: Ratio of distances covered = Ratio of speeds (when time is same)

name: Algebra — Identities & Shortcuts
description: Know all algebraic identities cold — they're SSC's favourite

sections.title: Must-Know Algebraic Identities
formulas:
(a+b)² = a² + 2ab + b²
(a-b)² = a² - 2ab + b²
a² - b² = (a+b)(a-b)
(a+b)³ = a³ + b³ + 3ab(a+b)
(a-b)³ = a³ - b³ - 3ab(a-b)
a³ + b³ = (a+b)(a² - ab + b²)
a³ - b³ = (a-b)(a² + ab + b²)
(a+b+c)² = a²+b²+c²+2ab+2bc+2ca

sections.title: If x + 1/x = k (Very Common in SSC!)
formulas:
x + 1/x = k
x² + 1/x² = k² - 2
x³ + 1/x³ = k³ - 3k  [or k(k²-3)]
x⁴ + 1/x⁴ = (x²+1/x²)² - 2 = (k²-2)² - 2

x - 1/x = √(k²-4) [if k>2]
x² - 1/x² = k × √(k²-4)
shortcuts: If x - 1/x = k: x² + 1/x² = k² + 2 | x³ - 1/x³ = k³ + 3k

sections.title: Linear Equations Shortcuts
shortcuts:
Cramer's Rule (2 equations):
    a₁x + b₁y = c₁ and a₂x + b₂y = c₂
    x = (c₁b₂ - c₂b₁)/(a₁b₂ - a₂b₁)
    y = (a₁c₂ - a₂c₁)/(a₁b₂ - a₂b₁)

No Solution: a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ ≠ c₁/c₂
Infinite Solutions: a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ = c₁/c₂

sections.title: Surds & Indices
formulas:
aᵐ × aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ
aᵐ / aⁿ = aᵐ⁻ⁿ
(aᵐ)ⁿ = aᵐⁿ
a⁰ = 1 (a≠0)
a⁻ⁿ = 1/aⁿ
aˡ/ⁿ = ⁿ√a
shortcuts:
To rationalize 1/(√a + √b): Multiply by (√a - √b)/(√a - √b)
    Result = (√a - √b)/(a - b)

name: Geometry — Lines, Triangles & Circles
description: Key theorems that SSC loves to test — with direct application

sections.title: Lines & Angles — Key Theorems
sections.content:
Alternate angles = equal
Corresponding angles = equal
Co-interior angles = supplementary (add to 180°)

sections.title: Circle Theorems — SSC Favorites
shortcuts:
Tangent-Secant Rule: If tangent PT and secant PAB from external point P:
    PT² = PA × PB (Power of a point)

sections.title: Quadrilaterals & Polygons
formulas:
Sum of interior angles of n-gon = (n-2) × 180°
Each interior angle of regular n-gon = (n-2)×180°/n
Number of diagonals = n(n-3)/2

name: Mensuration — 2D & 3D
description: All area, perimeter, volume and surface area formulas in one place

sections.title: 2D Shapes — Area & Perimeter
formulas:
Triangle by Heron's Formula (when 3 sides given):
s = (a+b+c)/2
Area = √[s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)]

sections.title: 3D Shapes — Volume & Surface Area
formulas:
Slant height of cone: l = √(h² + r²)
Diagonal of cuboid = √(l²+b²+h²)
Diagonal of cube = a√3

sections.title: Mensuration Tricks
shortcuts: Room problems: Floor area = l×b | 4 walls area = 2h(l+b) | Ceiling = l×b

When a square is converted to circle (same perimeter):
    4a = 2πr → r = 2a/π → Area increases (circle has more area)

When area increases by x%:
    Each side increases by: (√(100+x) - 10) × 10% approximately

sections.title: Important Values & Shortcuts
formulas:
If side doubles → area × 4
If side triples → area × 9
Ratio of areas = ratio of (sides)²

sections.title: Trigonometric Values Table (Must Memorize!)
shortcuts:
Memory trick for sin values: 0°→90° → √0/2, √1/2, √2/2, √3/2, √4/2 = 0, 1/2, 1/√2, √3/2, 1
    cos values are exactly sin in REVERSE order!

sections.title: Fundamental Identities
formulas:
sin²θ + cos²θ = 1  →  sin²θ = 1-cos²θ
1 + tan²θ = sec²θ  →  sec²θ - tan²θ = 1
1 + cot²θ = cosec²θ →  cosec²θ - cot²θ = 1

sec²θ - tan²θ = 1  →  (secθ+tanθ)(secθ-tanθ) = 1
If secθ + tanθ = k, then secθ - tanθ = 1/k
shortcuts:
Complementary angles:
    sin(90°-θ) = cosθ | cos(90°-θ) = sinθ
    tan(90°-θ) = cotθ | sec(90°-θ) = cosecθ

sections.title: Heights & Distances
shortcuts:
Standard results:
    If angle of elevation = 30° → Height = Distance/√3
    If angle of elevation = 45° → Height = Distance
    If angle of elevation = 60° → Height = Distance × √3

sections.title: Trig Simplification Tricks
formulas:
sin θ × cosec θ = 1
cos θ × sec θ = 1
tan θ × cot θ = 1
tan θ = sin θ / cos θ
shortcuts:
Step 1: Convert everything to sin and cos
Step 2: Use identities to simplify
Step 3: Look for (sin²+cos²=1) pattern

If sinθ + cosθ = k:
    sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 (always)
    sinθ × cosθ = (k²-1)/2
    sin³θ + cos³θ = k(1 - sinθcosθ) = k(1-(k²-1)/2)

name: Statistics & Data Interpretation
description: Mean, Median, Mode + DI shortcuts for quick calculation

sections.title: Mean, Median, Mode
formulas:
Mean = Sum of observations / n

Weighted Mean = Σ(w×x) / Σw

If all values increase by k: Mean increases by k
If all values multiplied by k: Mean multiplies by k

Arrange in order:
If n is odd: Median = ((n+1)/2)th term
If n is even: Median = avg of (n/2)th and (n/2+1)th

Mode = most frequent value
Relation: Mode = 3×Median - 2×Mean

sections.title: Data Interpretation Speed Tricks
shortcuts: For ratio questions: If A:B = x:y and total = T → A = T×x/(x+y)

For percentage questions on DI:
    Always find 10% first, then build up (20%=2×10%, 5%=10%÷2, 15%=10%+5%)

% change in DI: (New - Old)/Old × 100
    If old≈new, use approximation to save time

sections.title: Range, Variance & Standard Deviation
formulas:
Range = Maximum - Minimum

Variance = Σ(x - mean)² / n
Standard Deviation (SD) = √Variance

SD is never negative.
SD = 0 means all values are equal.
If each value increased by k: SD unchanged (only mean changes)
If each value multiplied by k: SD multiplies by |k|

sections.title: SSC Exam Strategy — Last Minute Tips
sections.content:
🏆 Do easy questions FIRST — Percentage, SI/CI, Ratio are fastest
⏱️ Time limit per question: 1.5 minutes max — skip if stuck
📐 Geometry & Mensuration — draw diagrams always, helps spot patterns
🔢 Use option elimination — plug in options for algebra problems
✅ Approximation in DI — never calculate exactly unless needed
🧠 LCM method for Time & Work — fastest approach
📊 Trig identities — convert to sin/cos first
🎯 Mark and review — come back to geometry theorems if uncertain

name: Sequence & Series — AP, GP & Special Series
description: SSC regularly tests AP/GP formulas and number pattern recognition

sections.title: Arithmetic Progression (AP)
formulas:
AP: a, a+d, a+2d, a+3d, ...
nth term:  Tₙ = a + (n-1)d
Sum of n terms: Sₙ = n/2 × [2a + (n-1)d]
                   = n/2 × (First + Last)
Middle term of AP = Arithmetic Mean of extremes
d = Common Difference = T₂ - T₁
shortcuts:
Trick — 3 numbers in AP: Take them as (a-d), a, (a+d). Sum = 3a.
4 numbers in AP: Take as (a-3d), (a-d), (a+d), (a+3d). Sum = 4a.

Sum of first n natural numbers: n(n+1)/2
Sum of first n odd numbers: n² (1+3+5+...+2n-1 = n²)
Sum of first n even numbers: n(n+1) (2+4+6+...+2n = n(n+1))

sections.title: Geometric Progression (GP)
formulas:
GP: a, ar, ar², ar³, ...
nth term: Tₙ = a × rⁿ⁻¹
Sum of n terms: Sₙ = a(rⁿ-1)/(r-1)  [if r≠1, r>1]
                   = a(1-rⁿ)/(1-r)  [if r<1]
Sum of infinite GP (|r|<1): S∞ = a/(1-r)
Common Ratio: r = T₂/T₁
shortcuts:
Trick — 3 numbers in GP: Take them as a/r, a, ar. Product = a³.
    Geometric Mean of a and b = √(ab)

sections.title: Number Series — Pattern Recognition
shortcuts:
Step 1: Check if differences form a pattern (AP or GP)
Step 2: If not, check ratios (GP series)
Step 3: If not, check squares/cubes ± constant

sections.title: Important Special Sums
formulas:
Sum of squares: 1²+2²+3²+...+n² = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6
Sum of cubes:  1³+2³+3³+...+n³ = [n(n+1)/2]²
Note: 1³+2³+...+n³ = (1+2+...+n)²

Sum of squares of first n EVEN numbers = 2n(n+1)(2n+1)/3
Sum of squares of first n ODD numbers = n(2n-1)(2n+1)/3

name: Simplification & Approximation
description: BODMAS, fractions, decimals, and fast calculation techniques

sections.title: BODMAS Rule — Order of Operations
shortcuts:
B — Brackets (solve innermost first: small → curly → square)
O — Orders (powers and roots)
D — Division (left to right)
M — Multiplication (left to right)
A — Addition
S — Subtraction

sections.title: Fast Calculation Tricks
shortcuts:
× 11: Write first digit, then add adjacent pairs, write last digit
    47 × 11 → 4, (4+7), 7 → 4, 11, 7 → carry 1 → 517

× 5: Divide by 2, shift decimal (or ×10÷2)
    246 × 5 = 2460/2 = 1230

sections.title: Fractions & Decimals Speed Methods
formulas:
0.ā = a/9         [single digit repeat]
0.ab̄ = (ab-a)/90   [one non-repeating, one repeating]
0.āb̄ = ab/99       [two digit repeat]
0.abcabc... = abc/999

Examples:
0.3333... = 3/9 = 1/3
0.142857... = 1/7 (recognise this!)
0.16̄ = (16-1)/90 = 15/90 = 1/6
shortcuts:
Cross-multiply to compare: a/b vs c/d → compare ad vs bc
    3/7 vs 4/9 → 3×9=27 vs 4×7=28 → 4/9 is bigger

sections.title: Approximation Techniques for SSC
shortcuts: Rule: For options 5+ apart, round aggressively. For close options, calculate carefully.

sections.title: Vedic Maths Tricks
shortcuts:
Example 43 × 47: (Tens same=4, Units add to 10: 3+7=10)
    Left part: 4×(4+1) = 20 | Right part: 3×7 = 21
    Answer: 2021

Rule: 123 ÷ 9 → Bring down 1, add to next: 1, 1+2=3, 3+3=6 → quotient 13, remainder 6
    Check: 9×13+6 = 117+6 = 123 ✓

name: Coordinate Geometry
description: Straight lines, distances, midpoints and slopes — SSC asks 2–4 questions

sections.title: Distance, Midpoint & Section Formula
formulas:
Distance between (x₁,y₁) and (x₂,y₂):
d = √[(x₂-x₁)² + (y₂-y₁)²]

Midpoint of two points:
M = ((x₁+x₂)/2 , (y₁+y₂)/2)

Section formula (divides in m:n internally):
P = ((mx₂+nx₁)/(m+n) , (my₂+ny₁)/(m+n))

Centroid of triangle (x₁,y₁)(x₂,y₂)(x₃,y₃):
G = ((x₁+x₂+x₃)/3 , (y₁+y₂+y₃)/3)

sections.title: Straight Lines — Everything
formulas:
Slope (m) = (y₂-y₁)/(x₂-x₁) = tan θ

Equation forms:
Slope-intercept: y = mx + c  (c = y-intercept)
Point-slope: y-y₁ = m(x-x₁)
Two-point: (y-y₁)/(y₂-y₁) = (x-x₁)/(x₂-x₁)
Intercept form: x/a + y/b = 1

Parallel lines: m₁ = m₂ (same slope)
Perpendicular lines: m₁ × m₂ = -1
shortcuts: Angle between two lines: tan θ = |m₁-m₂|/|1+m₁m₂|

sections.title: Distance from a Point to a Line
formulas:
Distance from point (x₁,y₁) to line ax+by+c=0:
d = |ax₁+by₁+c| / √(a²+b²)
Area = ½ |x₁(y₂-y₃) + x₂(y₃-y₁) + x₃(y₁-y₂)|
shortcuts: Collinear points check: Area = 0 means all 3 points lie on same line

sections.title: Circles in Coordinate Geometry
formulas:
Standard circle (centre at origin): x² + y² = r²
General circle (centre h,k): (x-h)² + (y-k)² = r²
General form: x²+y²+2gx+2fy+c = 0
Centre = (-g,-f) | Radius = √(g²+f²-c)

name: Mixtures, Alligation & Average
description: Alligation — the most powerful shortcut in SSC Maths

sections.title: Alligation Rule — The Cross Method
shortcuts:
THE CROSS:
          Cheaper (c)           Dearer (d)
                   \           /
                     Mean (m)
                   /           \
          (d-m)                 (m-c)
Ratio of quantities = (d-m) : (m-c)

sections.title: Replacement Problems
formulas:
If from a vessel of volume V containing liquid A,
x litres are removed and replaced with liquid B,
done n times:

Remaining A = V × (1 - x/V)ⁿ
Fraction of A remaining = ((V-x)/V)ⁿ
shortcuts: Ratio of milk:water finally = (V-x)ⁿ : Vⁿ - (V-x)ⁿ

sections.title: Average — All Formulas
formulas:
Average = Sum of all terms / Number of terms
Sum = Average × Number of terms

Average of consecutive natural nos from a to b = (a+b)/2
Average of first n natural nos = (n+1)/2
Average of first n even nos = n+1
Average of first n odd nos = n
Average of consecutive nos = middle number
shortcuts:
Weighted Average: If group 1 (n₁ people) has avg A₁ and group 2 (n₂) has avg A₂:
    Combined avg = (n₁A₁ + n₂A₂)/(n₁+n₂)

sections.title: Average Shortcut Tricks
shortcuts:
If wrong value used:
    Correct avg = Wrong avg + (Correct - Wrong)/n

If one number added/removed:
    New sum = Old sum ± new number
    New avg = New sum / New count

Cricket batting average trick:
    Avg increases by x runs → total extra runs = x × (no. of innings)
    Example: Avg goes from 40 to 42 after 10th innings → 10th innings score = old total + extra = 40×9 + extra
    New total = 42×10=420 | Old total = 40×9=360 | 10th innings = 420-360 = 60

sections.title: Age Problems
formulas:
Present age = x
Age n years ago = x - n
Age n years later = x + n

If A is n years older than B: A = B + n
Ratio of ages: Set up equation with ratio → solve
shortcuts: Golden tip: Always assume present age as variable. Write equations for past/future directly.
